whoWeAre.label: Who we are
whoWeAre.headline: Structure and clarity for the move ahead.
whoWeAre.subheadline: We help businesses move from uncertainty to structured growth.
whoWeAre.desc: Most growth problems are actually decision problems. We identify the constraints slowing you down and install the systems required to scale without chaos.

whyFardoy.label: Why Fardoy
whyFardoy.title: Beyond traditional consulting.
paragraphs: Fardoy is a strategic consultancy combining financial leadership, staffing strategy, and operational systems. We work alongside founders and leadership teams to execute the decisions that drive performance and growth.
Our approach is lean and intentional. We avoid consultancy theatre in favour of clear thinking, direct engagement, and effective solutions.
Business success is shaped by the decisions made day to day – whether optimising operations, scaling, or restructuring. This is where Fardoy brings practical insight and delivers measurable value.

stats.label: Combined experience across strategy, finance, and technology
Across M&A, fundraising, and digital transformation
Aligning strategy with execution, not just recommendations
Building workforce strategy and scalable infrastructure

principles.label: Principles
principles.title: The challenger consultancy, without the theatre.

cards.title: Define the real constraint
cards.desc: We separate symptoms from the decision that actually controls momentum.

cards.title: Choose what not to do
cards.desc: Growth improves when the business has fewer priorities with stronger ownership.

cards.title: Install execution rhythm
cards.desc: We turn strategy into review cycles, SOPs, dashboards, and decision rights.

cards.title: Make progress visible
cards.desc: The result is clearer accountability, better efficiency, and growth that can be managed.

leadership.desc2: The focus is a tighter route from decision to implementation.

steps.title: Diagnose
steps.desc: Identify the constraints, risks, and decisions that are slowing progress.

steps.title: Structure
steps.desc: Define a clear, focused path forward with aligned priorities and ownership.

steps.title: Execute
steps.desc: Support implementation with measurable outcomes and consistent progress.

items.q: What's the average cost of the project?
items.a: The price of anything is the amount of life you exchange for it. You are surrounded by simple, obvious solutions that can dramatically increase your income, power, influence and success. The problem is, you just don't see them.
Typically, engagements range from 3 to 6 months depending on the complexity of the transformation and the scale of the implementation required.
We follow a three-stage process: Diagnostic (clarifying constraints), Strategy (defining the move), and Execution (installing the systems to deliver).

items.q: What I can expect on our first call?
items.a: A peer-level discussion about your business constraints. We don't do pitch decks; we do diagnostics. We'll explore if Fardoy is the right fit for your current stage.

cta.label: NEXT STEP
cta.headline: Have a decision to make?
cta.subline: Focused consultation to understand your position, constraints, and next steps.

footer.tagline: Strategy consultancy for startups, SMEs, and scaling businesses.

hero.label: Capabilities
hero.title: Where we deliver impact
hero.subheadline: For businesses facing complexity, transition, or growth, we bring structure to the decisions that shape what comes next.

overview.label: What we do
overview.headline: Four areas focused on building, optimising, and scaling your business.

services.title: Startup Foundations.
services.desc: We help early-stage businesses move from idea to a structured, investment-ready position. This includes defining the business model, shaping the go-to-market approach, and establishing the financial and strategic foundations required for growth and investment.
situations: The idea is strong, but the business lacks a clear plan
Early interest exists, but the business lacks a defined model
The business lacks readiness for investment
focus: Establishing the business model, including financials, strategic narrative, and customer profiling
outcomes: A clear, investable business model
Defined route to market and target customer focus
Increased confidence in investor discussions
Financial clarity to support better decision-making
Early growth supported by structure, not assumptions

services.title: Performance & Profitability.
services.desc: We identify where value is being lost across cost, operations, and decision-making. We address inefficiencies and restore the financial visibility required to improve performance and profitability.
situations: Margins are declining despite increased activity
Costs are increasing without a clear return
Teams are busy, but outcomes remain inconsistent
Financial performance is unclear or difficult to track
Decision-making is reactive rather than structured
outcomes: Clear visibility over performance, costs, and margins
Identified and reduced sources of inefficiency
More consistent and predictable operational outcomes
Improved control over profitability and resource allocation
Better, faster decision-making supported by financial clarity

services.title: Growth Strategy.
services.desc: We help establish a clear growth plan, with defined steps to achieve business outcomes.
situations: Growth is happening, but customer experience is declining
The team is stretched or misaligned
The direction of growth is unclear or keeps changing
Expansion decisions feel reactive rather than planned
Growth is creating operational strain and internal friction
focus: Setting clear strategic priorities and execution plans
Aligning growth with operational and financial capacity
outcomes: A clear, actionable growth direction
Alignment between strategy, teams, and execution
More controlled and predictable scaling
Reduced friction as the business grows
Better, faster decisions supported by clear priorities

services.title: Digital Systems.
services.desc: We turn workflows and routines into scalable systems. We align your software and digital infrastructure with the business model to improve efficiency, consistency, and delivery.
situations: Processes are manual, inconsistent, or difficult to scale
Multiple tools are in use without clear purpose or structure
Execution depends on manual intervention or key individuals
Reporting and performance tracking are fragmented
The business lacks a website or digital presence aligned with its positioning and growth objectives
focus: Website design and development aligned with positioning and growth
Operational frameworks and standard operating procedures
AI adoption and automation where relevant
outcomes: Operations that are structured and scalable
Reduced dependency on manual intervention and key individuals
More efficient workflows and clearer accountability
Integrated systems that support consistent execution
A digital presence that supports growth and attracts the right clients

closing.label: NEXT STEP
closing.headline: Make the right decision, at the right time.
closing.subheadline: Focused consultation to understand your position and next steps.

hero.label: About
hero.headline: Clarity as a competitive edge.
hero.subheadline: We move businesses from discussion to decision. By turning complexity into clear direction, we help leaders act with confidence and build growth on financial and operational discipline.
hero.desc: We focus on what matters, and remove what doesn’t.

approach.label: Approach
approach.title: How we think.
paragraphs: We focus on identifying what is actually driving performance – and what is getting in the way.
This requires clear judgement: separating signal from noise, and focusing on what will materially move the business forward.

From there, we define clear priorities and the structure required to move forward, whether that involves financial clarity, operational focus, or alignment across teams and systems.

The objective is simple: fewer assumptions, better decisions, and a more direct path to execution.

difference.label: Our Difference
difference.title: Senior judgment, without unnecessary theatre.
difference.headline: Fardoy is intentionally lean and senior-led. We work directly on the problem, without layers, handovers, or unnecessary process.
difference.desc: Engagements are led by experienced consultants, with specialist expertise brought in only where it improves the outcome.

points.desc: Direct access to senior judgement throughout the engagement
points.title: Founder-led Engagement
Specialist expertise, where it matters
points.desc: Additional expertise brought in only where it improves the outcome

points.title: Strategy connected to execution
points.desc: Decisions translated into clear, actionable outcomes

founders.name: Shervin Fard
bio: Shervin works with founders and leadership teams on talent strategy, organisational design, and workforce planning.

He builds the staffing capability and foundations required for businesses to operate and scale with confidence, aligning talent with commercial priorities.

His experience includes managing global teams, bringing structure, accountability, and performance discipline across multiple sectors.

founders.name: Marzieh Fard
bio: Marzieh works with founders, leadership teams, and investors on financial leadership, strategic planning, and business transformation.

She enables better commercial decision-making by bringing clarity to financial performance, structure, and planning during periods of growth, restructuring, and investment.

Her experience includes senior finance roles across various sectors, bringing a disciplined approach to performance, planning, and scalable growth.

info.quote: Strategy is about making choices, trade-offs; it's about deliberately choosing to be different.

Identification of the key constraint and opportunities
expect.subcallout: Designed to bring clarity and define what to do next.

items.q: When should we speak to Fardoy?
items.a: When decisions feel unclear, growth slows, or execution becomes inconsistent.

items.q: Do you work with early-stage startups?
items.a: Yes, especially when founders need structure, financial clarity, and investor readiness.

items.q: Do you support execution or only strategy?
items.a: Both. Strategy is only useful when it can be implemented.
It depends on the scope, but every engagement is shaped around a defined business need.

items.q: What industries do you work with?
items.a: We have experience across SaaS, finance, technology, hospitality, talent strategy, and emerging sectors.
Yes. We support businesses operating across different markets.

enquiries.location: Strategically agile. Serving global markets from the UK.

engagements.desc2: This allows us to understand your business, your constraints, and whether there is a strong fit.

partnerships.desc1: For partnerships, collaborations, or general enquiries, feel free to reach out directly.
partnerships.desc2: We aim to respond within 1–2 business days.

sections.title: 1. Who we are
sections.content: Fardoy Strategic Consultancy is a UK-based consultancy providing strategic, financial, and operational advisory services. We act as the data controller for the personal data we collect.

sections.title: 2. Information we collect
sections.content:
We may collect and process the following information:
Contact details (name, email address, telephone number)
Business information (company name, role, sector)
Information submitted through enquiry forms or consultations
Technical data (IP address, browser type, usage data)

sections.title: 3. How we use your information
sections.content:
We use your information to:
Respond to enquiries and provide consultations
Deliver and improve our services
Communicate with you regarding our services
Maintain internal records and operational processes
Comply with legal and regulatory obligations

sections.title: 4. Lawful basis for processing
sections.content:
We process personal data under the following lawful bases:
Legitimate interests – to operate and improve our business
Contractual necessity – where we provide services to you
Consent – where required (e.g. marketing communications)

sections.title: 5. Data sharing
sections.content:
We do not sell your personal data. We may share data with:
Professional advisers (e.g. legal, accounting)
Service providers supporting our operations (e.g. hosting, analytics)
All third parties are required to handle data securely and in accordance with applicable laws.

sections.title: 6. Data retention
sections.content:
We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to:
Fulfil the purposes outlined above
Comply with legal and regulatory obligations

sections.title: 7. Data security
sections.content: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect personal data from unauthorised access, loss, or misuse.

sections.title: 8. Your rights
sections.content:
Under UK GDPR, you have the right to:
Access your personal data
Request correction or deletion
Restrict or object to processing
Withdraw consent (where applicable)
You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO).

sections.title: 9. Cookies
sections.content: Our website may use cookies and analytics tools to improve user experience. You can manage cookie preferences through your browser settings.

sections.title: 10. Contact
sections.content: For any privacy-related queries, contact: info@fardoy.com

termsPage.title: Terms of Use
termsPage.intro: By accessing and using this website, you agree to comply with these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the website.

sections.title: 1. About this website
sections.content: This website is operated by Fardoy Strategic Consultancy (Fardoy Limited). It provides information about our services and allows users to make enquiries.

sections.title: 2. Use of the website
sections.content:
You agree to use this website:
For lawful purposes only
In a way that does not infringe the rights of others
Without attempting to disrupt or compromise the site

sections.title: 3. Intellectual property
sections.content: All content on this website (text, design, graphics, branding) is owned by Fardoy Limited unless otherwise stated. You may not reproduce, distribute, or use content without prior written permission.

sections.title: 4. No reliance on content
sections.content: The content on this website is provided for general information only. It does not constitute professional advice, and should not be relied upon as such.

sections.title: 5. Limitation of liability
sections.content: We make reasonable efforts to ensure accuracy, but we do not guarantee that content is complete or up to date. We are not liable for any loss arising from reliance on this website.

sections.title: 6. Third-party links
sections.content: This website may include links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for the content or practices of those sites.

sections.title: 7. Availability
sections.content: We do not guarantee uninterrupted access to the website. We may update, suspend, or withdraw the site at any time.

sections.title: 8. Governing law
sections.content: These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales.

sections.title: 9. Contact
sections.content: For any queries regarding these terms: hello@fardoy.com
